competitor_patterns: Rudy Project ranks #1 for 'best cycling glasses 2026' with 4,686 words on their homepage — they win on domain authority and brand breadth, not dedicated blog content; their H2s are generic ('Our products', 'Latest drops'), meaning a focused editorial page can outrank them

Trigearlab dominates 'road cycling glasses review' with a 9,735-word buyer's guide featuring 10 product picks — word count and review depth are the clear ranking lever; their 2024-dated title is a vulnerability we can exploit with a 2026 update

Top-ranking content consistently combines a comparison table, a buyer's guide section, and reader reviews in one page — our shorter posts (1,000–1,300 words) lack the depth to compete with these multi-format pages

Competitors like Rudy Project and Trigearlab include FAQs (confirmed has_faq: true) — this directly feeds Google's FAQ rich results and AI Overview snippets, giving extra SERP real estate; our FAQ-schema coverage is only 33%

DHgate ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with product-catalogue content, not editorial — this signals Google treats the query as commercial/transactional, meaning a hybrid page (guide + direct product CTA) can displace a marketplace listing
our_gaps: Critical word-count gap on short posts: 22 of our 41 pages are under 1,400 words — Trigearlab's #1-ranking post is 9,735 words; even our longest posts (~5,500 words on the SunGod comparison) sit well below the competitive ceiling for high-volume 'best cycling glasses' queries

Only 33% of pages have FAQ schema — competitors with FAQ schema win additional SERP features and AI Overview citations; the 27+ pages without faq_schema are leaving structured-data click opportunities on the table

No dedicated 'road cycling glasses review' or long-form buyer's guide targeting the exact 'road cycling glasses review' query — Trigearlab owns position #1 with a review-format page and we have no direct editorial competitor to it

Our comparison posts focus on brand vs brand (Velluto vs X) but top-ranking content is category-level ('best cycling sunglasses 2026' with 10 picks) — we lack a true 10-product roundup that could rank for the highest-volume head terms

Meta titles are underoptimised for typo-variant traffic: 'veluto' (235 impressions, 0.9% CTR, position 5.6) is almost certainly landing on the brand homepage rather than a dedicated page — there is no typo-redirect or title tag capturing this intent
keyword_opportunities: road cycling glasses review 2026 — Trigearlab ranks #1 with a 2024-dated page; a fresh 2026 version with updated picks and Velluto StradaPro as a featured option is a direct displacement opportunity

interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026 — DHgate holds #1 with a product catalogue; an editorial guide ('Best Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Tested & Ranked') with a comparison table would outrank a marketplace page for informational intent

lightweight cycling sunglasses 25g — no competitor is explicitly targeting the 25g weight claim as a search query; this ultra-specific feature keyword has low competition and directly maps to StradaPro's strongest differentiator

cycling glasses 30 day trial — zero competitor content addresses risk-free trial as a search angle; buyers researching 'try before you buy cycling glasses' or 'cycling glasses money back guarantee' have no dedicated editorial answer

anti-fog cycling glasses climbs 2026 — we have thin posts on this topic (~1,032 words) but no deep guide; the query bridges summer riding season demand with a specific StradaPro feature, and no competitor has a definitive page on it
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 3,500 words for any post competing on head terms ('best cycling glasses 2026', 'best cycling sunglasses 2026', 'road cycling glasses review') — match Trigearlab's depth benchmark of ~9,000 words for the definitive roundup post

Every post must include: (1) a comparison table, (2) an FAQ section with minimum 4 Q&As marked up with FAQ schema, (3) at least one numbered list of criteria or picks — this matches the multi-format pattern of every top-ranking competitor page

Lead with a specific, data-anchored claim in the H1 and first paragraph — e.g. '25g, tool-free lens swap, 30-day trial: here is why the StradaPro beats Oakley Radar at half the price in 2026' — this feeds AI Overview extraction and differentiates the snippet

For 'oakley radar alternative' (position 4.6, only 1 click from 67 impressions) write a dedicated post titled exactly 'Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: 5 Cycling Glasses That Beat It on Weight & Price' — the near-top position means a title and intro optimised for this exact query will dramatically lift CTR

Include a 'Who This Is For' section near the top of every post — one short paragraph each for the serious road cyclist, the commuter, and the budget-conscious buyer; this matches buyer-guide patterns that win featured snippets and AI Overview citations

Add a 'Verdict' H2 near the end of every post that explicitly answers the query in 2–3 sentences — AI systems (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overview) extract direct answers; a clearly labelled verdict section increases citation probability

For all new posts on interchangeable-lens or anti-fog topics, embed a product demo description (or link to video) explaining the click-in lens swap in under 10 seconds — this tactile differentiator is not addressed by any competitor and reduces purchase hesitation
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all 27+ pages currently missing it — prioritise the 15+ short posts (1,000–1,300 words) where adding 4 FAQ items with schema is the fastest way to gain SERP real estate without a full rewrite; at 33% coverage this is the single highest-leverage structural fix

Add a comparison table to every page currently missing one — 18 pages have table=NO; a simple 5-column table (Brand / Weight / Lens System / Price / Trial Policy) takes under 30 minutes to add and is confirmed as a top-3 format feature among ranking competitors

Ensure every page's meta title contains the year '2026' and a power word ('Best', 'Tested', 'Ranked', 'vs') — GSC data shows our 2026-suffixed queries ('best cycling sunglasses 2026' at 3.5% CTR, 'best cycling glasses 2026' at 4.1% CTR) outperform non-year variants by 1.5–2× CTR
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store 2026 | Why: Captures the misspelling intent (235 impressions) while surfacing the correct brand name and year; current ~0.9% CTR at pos 5.6 suggests the title does not match the searcher's navigational intent — adding 'Official Store' signals authority and increases navigational click confidence

best cycling glasses → New title: Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Tested & Ranked | Why: Current CTR 2.7% at pos 8.4 is low for a branded-adjacent query; adding '2026', 'Tested' and 'Ranked' matches the proven higher-CTR pattern of our top performers ('best cycling sunglasses 2026' at 3.5%, 'best cycling glasses 2026' at 4.1%) and signals fresh editorial content

oakley radar alternative → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: Lighter & Cheaper | Why: CTR is 1.5% from position 4.6 — nearly top 3 but almost no one clicks; the current title presumably does not contain 'Oakley Radar Alternative' as an exact phrase; leading with the exact query match plus two concrete benefit hooks ('Lighter & Cheaper') directly addresses the comparison intent and should 3–4× CTR

oakley alternative → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses 2026 – Top 5 | Why: 1.7% CTR at pos 7.6 is underperforming for a query with 60 impressions; appending 'Cycling Glasses' narrows to the right audience, '2026' signals recency, and 'Top 5' sets a listicle expectation that raises click-through for comparison-intent searchers
content_quick_wins: velluto (pos 4.7) → Topic: Brand homepage / main landing page optimisation | Add: Ensure the page title tag reads 'Velluto Cycling Glasses – StradaPro | Official Store' (exact brand match), add a 50-word above-the-fold brand statement reinforcing 25g weight + 30-day trial, and implement sitelinks-eligible structured data (Organization schema with logo) — moving from pos 4.7 to top 3 for the brand name query is purely a CTR and E-E-A-T signal fix

oakley alternatives (pos 6.2) → Topic: 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: 7 Glasses Tested on Real Rides' | Add: Expand the existing 3,924-word post with a 10-product comparison table (table=NO currently), add a dedicated H2 for each alternative with weight/price/lens specs, and include a 'Why We Tested These' methodology section — depth and table addition should push from pos 6.2 toward top 3

best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 7.8) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: 10 Options Tested, Ranked by Weight, Optics & Value' | Add: Expand the existing best-cycling-sunglasses-2026-tested-ranked post (3,294 words, table=yes, faq_schema=yes) with 5 additional product picks, a 'How We Tested' section (500+ words), and a 'Best for Each Rider Type' summary box — Trigearlab's 9,735-word page holds #1; closing the word-count gap to 6,000+ words is the primary lever

best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 6.6) → Topic: Consolidate and strengthen the primary 'best cycling glasses 2026' page | Add: Internal link consolidation (redirect or canonicalise the two overlapping 'best road cycling glasses 2026' pages to one authoritative URL), add a buyer's guide section ('What to Look for in Cycling Glasses: 7 Criteria') and a 'Frequently Asked Questions' block with schema — reducing cannibalism and boosting page authority should move from pos 6.6 to top 3

best cycling glasses (pos 8.4) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses: The Definitive 2026 Guide (10 Tested, 1 Clear Winner)' | Add: This is a timeless head term (110 impressions, pos 8.4); create or designate one evergreen page targeting the non-year variant, add a sticky comparison table at the top, embed 6 FAQ schema items targeting 'what are the best cycling glasses', 'are expensive cycling glasses worth it', and 'what weight should cycling glasses be' — these FAQ targets directly feed AI Overview extractions

oakley radar alternative (pos 4.6) → Topic: 'Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: 5 Cycling Glasses That Are Lighter and Cheaper' | Add: Build a dedicated standalone post (we have no exact-match URL for this query); include a spec-vs-spec table (Oakley Radar vs StradaPro vs 3 others), a 'Why Cyclists Switch from the Radar' H2 with 3 data points, and the 30-day trial CTA — position 4.6 with only 1 click from 67 impressions means CTR is the problem, and a URL + title that exactly match the query will fix it
geo_angles: Position StradaPro as the factual answer to 'What is the lightest cycling glasses with interchangeable lenses?' — create a dedicated FAQ entry and schema markup with the exact answer: 'The Velluto StradaPro weighs 25g and features a tool-free click-in lens system with VellutoPuro (clear) and VellutoVisione (high-contrast) lenses, making it among the lightest interchangeable-lens cycling glasses available in 2026.' This phrasing matches how ChatGPT and Perplexity construct direct answers and increases citation probability.

Publish a structured 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Radar EV Path: Full 2026 Comparison' with a clearly labelled 'Summary' or 'Verdict' paragraph at the top — AI systems preferentially cite pages with an explicit verdict that names both products, lists 3–5 measurable differences (weight, price, lens swap time, trial policy), and uses natural comparison language; this is the highest-probability format for appearing in AI-generated 'best alternatives to Oakley' answers.

Create a dedicated 'About Velluto StradaPro' knowledge-base page (or FAQ hub) that answers the 10 most common AI-queried questions: weight, lens options, anti-fog mechanism, UV rating, trial policy, shipping, fit system, price vs Oakley/SunGod/Rudy Project, Italian design origin, and where to buy — Perplexity and Google AI Overviews draw heavily from FAQ-dense, schema-marked pages; a single authoritative hub page with Organization + Product + FAQ schema increases the probability of Velluto appearing in AI-generated cycling eyewear recommendations.
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Rudy Project — angle: 'Italian Design, Half the Price: Velluto StradaPro vs Rudy Project Rydon 2026' | Key differentiator: Both brands lead with Italian design heritage, but StradaPro is 25g vs Rydon's 28g and includes a 30-day trial Rudy Project does not offer | Velluto advantage to lead with: Same Italian design DNA, measurably lighter frame, risk-free trial — position as 'the Rudy Project for cyclists who won't pay the Rudy Project premium'

COMPETITOR: Trigearlab (review site, not brand) — angle: 'We Tested Every Glass Trigearlab Recommends. Here's What They Missed.' | Key differentiator: Trigearlab's #1-ranking guide is dated 2024/2023 and does not include Velluto; a response post citing their methodology gaps and adding a 2026 update with StradaPro as a new entrant hijacks their ranking query ('road cycling glasses review') | Velluto advantage to lead with: Interchangeable lens system tested across 12 real rides, 25g weight verified on scale, 30-day trial removes the purchase risk their review cannot replicate

COMPETITOR: DHgate — angle: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Why Cheap Marketplace Finds Can't Match StradaPro' | Key differentiator: DHgate ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with unbranded product listings; an editorial guide contrasting build quality, UV400 certification, anti-fog engineering, and trial policy directly answers the comparison intent searchers have when they land on DHgate | Velluto advantage to lead with: UV400 certified, built-in anti-fog (not a coating), 30-day trial, Italian design — vs unverified marketplace specs with no returns guarantee
